       "<INSTRUCTIONS>\n" +
       "# Self-Review Standards\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Be my brutally honest strategic advisor. Call out excuses, avoidance, and wasted time with opportunity cost analysis. Don't soften feedback on flawed code or architecture. Push for simplicity when I'm overcomplicating.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Apply this to your own work:\n" +
       "- Verify your changes solve the stated problem\n" +
       "- Confirm refactored components still integrate\n" +
       "- Challenge your own assumptions\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Search the web when creating plans for current best practices.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Truth-First Reasoning\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Do not agree by default. Verify claims against evidence, code, docs, or logic.\n" +
       "- Correct false assumptions directly; separate partial truth from error.\n" +
       "- Say \"unknown\" when evidence is insufficient.\n" +
       "- Start claim, plan, review, or decision responses with a verdict when useful: Correct, Incorrect, Partially correct, Unknown, Bad approach, or Better approach available.\n" +
       "- Do not implement bad ideas silently. Explain the flaw and use the smallest correct fix.\n" +
       "- Inspect the real code path before accepting a diagnosis.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Engagement Modes\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Strategic (default): Challenge ideas, push back on bad decisions.\n" +
       "Triggers: planning, architecture, code review, \"what do you think\"\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Debugging: Answer direct questions, support investigation, don't hijack.\n" +
       "Triggers: \"I'm debugging X\", active troubleshooting\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Collaborative: Present findings, wait for direction before proceeding.\n" +
       "Triggers: \"we are working together\", discovery/analysis\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Always honest. In debugging/collaborative modes, answer what was asked.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Implementation vs. Analysis\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Analysis (\"suggest\", \"analyze\", \"review\", \"investigate\"): Findings only, no code changes\n" +
       "- Implementation (\"fix\", \"implement\", \"change\", \"update\"): Make the changes\n" +
       "- Ambiguous: Ask before proceeding\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Before implementing, search for existing implementations or patterns first.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Code Quality\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Simplest solution first. Add complexity only when requested or necessary\n" +
       "- Simple flags over config objects. Conditionals over abstraction layers\n" +
       "- No premature optimization or unneeded flexibility\n" +
       "- Validate consuming code when refactoring APIs\n" +
       "- Check for existing files/services before creating new ones\n" +
       "- Direct solutions, not lengthy analysis. No alternatives unless asked\n" +
       "- Implementation over architecture unless architecture is the topic\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Testing\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Default: TDD unless specified otherwise.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Search\n" +
       "\n" +
       "**Code**: use `rg` (ripgrep):\n" +
       "- Content: `rg \"pattern\"`, filter with `--type py` or `-g \"*.sh\"`\n" +
       "- Files: `rg --files -g \"*.ts\"`\n" +
       "- Context: `rg -C3 \"pattern\"` for surrounding lines\n" +
       "\n" +
       "**Docs**: use qmd for markdown:\n" +

       "- Fallback: `qmd search` or `qmd vsearch`\n" +
       "- Read with `qmd get` or cat\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Communication\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Be direct, evidence-based, and specific.\n" +
       "- Do not use agreement phrases unless the claim has been verified.\n" +
       "- State corrections plainly, without fake agreement.\n" +
       "- Never write em dashes. Use commas, periods, parentheses, or colons instead.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Shell Execution\n" +
       "\n" +

       "# Codebase Knowledge Graph (codebase-memory-mcp)\n" +

       "## Priority Order\n" +

       "--- project-doc ---\n" +
       "\n" +
       "# AGENTS.md\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Shared instructions for any coding agent (human or AI) working in this repo.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "The product spec of record is `docs/prd.md`. Read it before making non-trivial\n" +
       "decisions. This file is a quick-reference companion, not a replacement.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Project\n" +
       "\n" +
       "**ReviewLens AI** — a review intelligence portal. An analyst pastes one B2B\n" +
       "software-review platform URL (GetApp primary, G2 smoke-test, manual import\n" +
       "fallback), the app ingests the public review corpus into PostgreSQL, then lets\n" +
       "the analyst ask grounded questions answered *only* from the ingested reviews,\n" +
       "with supporting review IDs and explicit refusals for out-of-scope questions.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "This is a hiring exercise. Three explicit review criteria: (1) deployed product,\n" +
       "(2) evidence-grounded answers with guardrails, (3) committed AI transcripts under\n" +
       "`/ai-transcripts`. Do not optimize for generic chatbot behavior.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Stack — do not deviate without a reason\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Rails 8.1.x monolith, Ruby 4.0.5 (`.ruby-version`).\n" +
       "- PostgreSQL only (primary + Solid Queue / Solid Cache / Solid Cable as separate\n" +
       "  DBs in production). No Redis.\n" +
       "- Hotwire/Turbo + Stimulus, ERB views, Tailwind CSS, Importmap JS.\n" +
       "- Active Job via Solid Queue. Two processes: `web` (bin/rails server) and\n" +
       "  `worker` (bin/jobs).\n" +
       "- OpenAI for AI calls, using **Structured Outputs** for schema-bound JSON. RubyLLM\n" +
       "- Deploy: Kamal + DigitalOcean, Docker. `config/deploy.yml`, `.kamal/secrets`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Hard \"no\" list from the PRD: no React, no Next, no LangChain, no full vector DB\n" +
       "unless everything else is done, no multi-platform, no user accounts, no scheduled\n" +
       "crawls, no auth.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Commands\n" +
       "\n" +
       "```bash\n" +
       "bin/setup            # bundle install + db:prepare (+ optional --reset)\n" +
       "bin/dev              # web (puma) + tailwind watcher via foreman, PORT default 3000\n" +
       "\n" +
       "bin/rails test                 # minitest, parallelized\n" +
       "bin/rails test:system          # capybara + selenium-webdriver\n" +
       "bin/rails db:test:prepare      # run before tests in CI\n" +
       "bin/rails db:prepare           # create + migrate dev DB\n" +
       "\n" +
       "bin/rubocop          # Omakase style (rubocop-rails-omakase), use -f github for CI\n" +
       "bin/brakeman         # static security scan\n" +
       "bin/bundler-audit   # gem CVE audit (config in config/bundler-audit.yml)\n" +
       "bin/importmap audit  # JS dependency audit\n" +
       "bin/ci               # local CI runner helper\n" +
       "```\n" +
       "\n" +
       "CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) runs four jobs: `scan_ruby`, `scan_js`, `lint`,\n" +
       "`test`, `system-test`. Postgres is provided as a service with\n" +
       "`DATABASE_URL=postgres://****@localhost:5432`. Keep all five green.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "### Env vars (production)\n" +
       "\n" +
       "`RAILS_MASTER_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_MODEL`,\n" +
       "`RAILS_ENV=production`. Secrets never go in code; use credentials/`.kamal/secrets`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Architecture\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Keep service boundaries as specified in `docs/prd.md` section 13. Do not flatten\n" +
       "them into controllers/models.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "```\n" +
       "app/services/\n" +
       "  review_platforms/\n" +
       "    detector.rb            # url -> adapter; raises UnsupportedPlatform\n" +
       "    base_adapter.rb        # contract below\n" +
       "    getapp_adapter.rb      # primary\n" +
       "    g2_adapter.rb          # smoke-test only, do not fight anti-bot\n" +
       "    manual_adapter.rb      # fallback, insurance policy, build early\n" +
       "\n" +
       "  ingestion/\n" +
       "    fetcher.rb             # whitelisted hosts, 2 redirects max, 10s timeout, 5MB cap\n" +
       "    parser_result.rb\n" +
       "    importer.rb            # dedup by external_review_id else content_hash; truncate 5000 chars\n" +
       "    summary_builder.rb     # count, avg rating, histogram, sentiment, field coverage, warnings\n" +
       "\n" +
       "  ai/\n" +
       "    json_client.rb         # OpenAI structured-outputs wrapper\n" +
       "    schemas.rb             # BATCH_SUMMARY, SCOPE_GUARD, ANSWER\n" +
       "    prompts.rb\n" +
       "\n" +
       "  review_analysis/\n" +
       "    batch_summarizer.rb    # BATCH_SIZE 30 reviews per insight batch\n" +
       "    scope_guard.rb         # prompt-driven + cheap regex pre-filter (regex not the sole guard)\n" +
       "    context_builder.rb     # summary + batch insights + keyword-matched reviews (cap 50)\n" +
       "    question_answerer.rb   # guard -> context -> answer; refuse if guard denies\n" +
       "```\n" +
       "\n" +
       "### Adapter contract (every platform adapter implements)\n" +
       "\n" +
       "`valid_url?(url)`, `external_id(url)`, `canonical_url(url)`,\n" +
       "`fetch_pages(url)`, `parse_reviews(page_html)`, `parse_product_metadata(page_html)`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Normalized review hash: `external_review_id`, `content_hash`, `source_url`,\n" +
       "`rating`, `sentiment`, `title`, `body`, `reviewer_label`, `reviewer_role`,\n" +
       "`reviewer_company_size`, `review_date`, `raw_payload`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "### Fetcher hard constraints\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Whitelisted hosts only: `getapp.com` / `www.getapp.com`, `g2.com` /\n" +

       "login, paywall, CAPTCHA, or bot blocks. If G2 blocks, stop — do not fight it;\n" +
       "ship only the adapter that passes its smoke test.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "### Ingestion flow\n" +
       "\n" +
       "`POST /products` -> detect platform, validate URL, create `Product` ->\n" +
       "create `IngestionRun` -> enqueue `IngestionJob` -> redirect to product show.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "`IngestReviewsJob`: `fetching` -> `parsing` -> import -> summary ->\n" +
       "`summarizing` -> batch summarize -> `ready`. On any failure: `product.status =\n" +
       "failed`, `ingestion_run.status = failed`. Status is a string enum:\n" +
       "`pending fetching parsing summarizing ready failed`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Data model (PostgreSQL)\n" +
       "\n" +
       "`products`, `ingestion_runs`, `reviews`, `insight_batches`, `chat_messages`.\n" +
       "Full column lists and indexes are in `docs/prd.md` section 12. Implement status\n" +
       "enums as string enums. Key uniqueness:\n" +
       "`reviews [product_id, external_review_id]` (where not null) and\n" +
       "`reviews [product_id, content_hash]`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Current state: no migrations, no schema, no models beyond `ApplicationRecord`.\n" +
       "Models to create: `Product`, `IngestionRun`, `Review`, `InsightBatch`,\n" +
       "`ChatMessage`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Routes\n" +
       "\n" +
       "```ruby\n" +
       "root \"products#new\"\n" +
       "resources :products, only: [:new, :create, :show] do\n" +
       "  resources :chat_messages, only: [:create]\n" +
       "end\n" +
       "```\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Guardrails — non-negotiable\n" +
       "\n" +
       "The Q&A system answers **only** from the ingested review corpus for the current\n" +
       "product/platform. Disallowed: other review platforms, competitor comparisons,\n" +
       "general world knowledge, current events, weather, market facts, advice not\n" +
       "grounded in the corpus, anything requiring external browsing.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "The guardrail must be **primarily system-prompt driven** (per the assignment),\n" +
       "with a cheap regex pre-filter as backup only. Refusal copy must be explicit and\n" +
       "graceful, naming the current platform. Suggested \"try blocked questions\" buttons\n" +
       "are part of the UI so the guardrail is obvious in the demo.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Required guardrail demo questions must all be refused:\n" +
       "`How do G2 reviews compare?`, `What is the current weather?`,\n" +
       "`Is this better than Zapier?`, `What are the latest sales numbers?`,\n" +
       "`What do Amazon reviews say?`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## AI design — three calls\n" +
       "\n" +
       "1. **Batch summarization** — 30 reviews/batch -> structured themes (pain points,\n" +
       "   praised features, feature requests, buyer objections, notable quotes) with\n" +
       "   supporting review IDs. Stored in `insight_batches`.\n" +
       "2. **Scope guard** — `{allowed, blocked_category, reason, safe_rewritten_question}`.\n" +
       "3. **Answer generation** — `{answer_markdown, confidence, supporting_review_ids, limitations}`.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Use OpenAI Structured Outputs so the app can parse responses reliably.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Testing rules\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Test saved HTML fixtures, never live scraping. Fixtures under\n" +
       "  `test/fixtures/files/` (e.g. `getapp_make.html`, `g2_notion.html`).\n" +
       "- Minitest. Default to TDD.\n" +
       "- Build the scrape probe (`script/scrape_probe.rb`) to record fixtures before\n" +
       "  writing adapters.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## Conventions\n" +
       "\n" +
       "- Ruby styling: Omakase (`bin/rubocop`). Match existing style, don't introduce a\n" +
       "  house config.\n" +
       "- No secrets in code. Use Rails credentials and `.kamal/secrets` (gitignored).\n" +
       "- Keep it boring: simple services, plain ERB, no premature abstraction. The PRD\n" +
       "  explicitly warns against scope creep toward a \"generic data ingestion platform.\"\n" +
       "- Commit real AI sessions to `/ai-transcripts/` (including dead ends and failed\n" +
       "  scraper attempts). Redact secrets only. Mention this in the README.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "## When in doubt\n" +
       "\n" +
       "Read `docs/prd.md`. It is opinionated on purpose; follow it unless you have a\n" +
       "concrete reason to diverge, and state that reason in the PR or commit.\n" +
       "\n" +
       "</INSTRUCTIONS>"},

require "json"
require "pathname"

ROOT = Pathname.new("/Users/dpaluy/.codex/worktrees/183b/ReviewLens")
OUT = ROOT.join("ai-transcripts")

CODEX_FILES = [
  "/Users/dpaluy/.codex/sessions/2026/06/25/rollout-2026-06-25T16-36-36-019f00b6-a03a-76b3-89ab-b476721a0148.jsonl",
  "/Users/dpaluy/.codex/sessions/2026/06/25/rollout-2026-06-25T22-09-06-019f01e7-088e-7761-abe0-a36dcf28ecc2.jsonl",
  "/Users/dpaluy/.codex/sessions/2026/06/25/rollout-2026-06-25T22-09-16-019f01e7-3097-7fe2-afe7-c08edf1760ad.jsonl",
  "/Users/dpaluy/.codex/sessions/2026/06/25/rollout-2026-06-25T22-09-28-019f01e7-5fe1-7193-a968-7169102e1169.jsonl",
  "/Users/dpaluy/.codex/sessions/2026/06/25/rollout-2026-06-25T22-09-39-019f01e7-8c46-7012-b00b-477806c75dc7.jsonl"
]

PI_FILES = Dir[File.expand_path("~/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-dpaluy-projects-ReviewLens--/*.jsonl")].sort
CLAUDE_FILES = Dir[File.expand_path("~/.claude/projects/-Users-dpaluy-projects-ReviewLens/*.jsonl")].sort

MAX_TEXT = 2_400
MAX_ITEMS_PER_FILE = 80

def redact(text)
  s = text.to_s.dup
  s.gsub!(/[A-Z0-9]{20,}/, "[REDACTED_LONG_IDENTIFIER]")
  s.gsub!(/\b(?:sk|ghp|gho|github_pat|xox[baprs])-[-A-Za-z0-9_]{8,}\b/, "[REDACTED_TOKEN]")
  s.gsub!(/\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b/, "[REDACTED_EMAIL]")
  s.gsub!(/OP_ACCOUNT=\S+/, "OP_ACCOUNT=[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/--account\s+"?[^"\s]+"?/, "--account [REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/op:\/\/[^\s"`]+/, "op://[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/(RAILS_MASTER_KEY|OPENAI_API_KEY|DIGITALOCEAN_API_TOKEN|POSTGRES_PASSWORD|DATABASE_URL)=\S+/, "\\1=[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/(password|api[_-]?key|token|credential|secret)(\s*[:=]\s*)([^\s`"']+)/i, "\\1\\2[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/\e\[[0-9;]*m/, "")
  s.gsub!(/[ \t]+$/, "")
  s
end

def truncate(text)
  s = redact(text.to_s)
  return s if s.length <= MAX_TEXT

  s[0, MAX_TEXT] + "\n[...truncated for artifact readability...]"
end

def content_to_text(content)
  case content
  when String
    content
  when Array
    content.filter_map do |item|
      case item
      when Hash
        case item["type"]
        when "text", "input_text", "output_text"
          item["text"]
        when "toolCall"
          args = item["arguments"] || item["input"] || {}
          "[tool call: #{item["name"]}]\n#{args.inspect}"
        when "toolResult"
          "[tool result]\n#{content_to_text(item["content"])}"
        when "image", "image/png"
          "[image payload omitted]"
        when "thinking"
          nil
        else
          item["text"] || item["content"] && content_to_text(item["content"])
        end
      else
        item.to_s
      end
    end.join("\n")
  when Hash
    content_to_text([content])
  else
    content.to_s
  end
end

def write_export(path, title, source_files)
  lines = []
  lines << "# #{title}"
  lines << ""
  lines << "Type: sanitized raw session export from local AI session logs."
  lines << "Generated: 2026-06-26."
  lines << "Project: ReviewLens."
  lines << ""
  lines << "Omitted: internal reasoning, encrypted reasoning payloads, binary/image payloads, and unrelated cache/config data."
  lines << "Redacted: tokens, API keys, account identifiers, email addresses, 1Password item references, and obvious secret values."
  lines << ""
  lines << "## Source Files"
  source_files.each { |f| lines << "- `#{f}`" }
  lines << ""
  yield lines
  File.write(path, lines.join("\n") + "\n")
end

def export_codex(files)
  write_export(OUT.join("2026-06-26-codex-session-export.md"), "Codex ReviewLens Session Export", files.select { |f| File.exist?(f) }) do |lines|
    files.each do |file|
      next unless File.exist?(file)
      count = 0
      lines << "## #{File.basename(file)}"
      File.foreach(file) do |line|
        begin
          item = JSON.parse(line)
        rescue JSON::ParserError
          next
        end

        ts = item["timestamp"]
        type = item["type"]
        payload = item["payload"] || {}

        if type == "session_meta"
          lines << ""
          lines << "Session: `#{payload["id"] || payload["session_id"]}`"
          lines << "CWD: `#{payload["cwd"]}`"
          lines << ""
          next

CLAUDE_FILES = Dir[File.expand_path("~/.claude/projects/-Users-dpaluy-projects-ReviewLens/*.jsonl")].sort

MAX_TEXT = 2_400
MAX_ITEMS_PER_FILE = 80

def redact(text)
  s = text.to_s.dup
  s.gsub!(/[A-Z0-9]{20,}/, "[REDACTED_LONG_IDENTIFIER]")
  s.gsub!(/\b(?:sk|ghp|gho|github_pat|xox[baprs])-[-A-Za-z0-9_]{8,}\b/, "[REDACTED_TOKEN]")
  s.gsub!(/\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b/, "[REDACTED_EMAIL]")
  s.gsub!(/OP_ACCOUNT=\S+/, "OP_ACCOUNT=[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/--account\s+"?[^"\s]+"?/, "--account [REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/op:\/\/[^\s"`]+/, "op://[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/(RAILS_MASTER_KEY|OPENAI_API_KEY|DIGITALOCEAN_API_TOKEN|POSTGRES_PASSWORD|DATABASE_URL)=\S+/, "\\1=[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/(password|api[_-]?key|token|credential|secret)(\s*[:=]\s*)([^\s`"']+)/i, "\\1\\2[REDACTED]")
  s.gsub!(/\e\[[0-9;]*m/, "")
  s.gsub!(/[ \t]+$/, "")
  s
end

def truncate(text)
  s = redact(text.to_s)
  return s if s.length <= MAX_TEXT

  "#{s[0, MAX_TEXT]}\n[...truncated for artifact readability...]"
end

def content_to_text(content)
  case content
  when String
    content
  when Array
    content.filter_map do |item|
      case item
      when Hash
        case item["type"]
        when "text", "input_text", "output_text"
          item["text"]
        when "toolCall"
          args = item["arguments"] || item["input"] || {}
          "[tool call: #{item["name"]}]\n#{args.inspect}"
        when "toolResult"
          "[tool result]\n#{content_to_text(item["content"])}"
        when "image", "image/png"
          "[image payload omitted]"
        when "thinking"
          nil
        else
          item["text"] || (item["content"] && content_to_text(item["content"]))
        end
      else
        item.to_s
      end
    end.join("\n")
  when Hash
    content_to_text([content])
  else
    content.to_s
  end
end

def write_export(path, title, source_files)
  lines = []
  lines << "# #{title}"
  lines << ""
  lines << "Type: sanitized raw session export from local AI session logs."
  lines << "Generated: 2026-06-26."
  lines << "Project: ReviewLens."
  lines << ""
  lines << "Omitted: internal reasoning, encrypted reasoning payloads, binary/image payloads, and unrelated cache/config data."
  lines << "Redacted: tokens, API keys, account identifiers, email addresses, 1Password item references, and obvious secret values."
  lines << ""
  lines << "## Source Files"
  source_files.each { |f| lines << "- `#{f}`" }
  lines << ""
  yield lines
  File.write(path, lines.join("\n") + "\n")
end

def export_codex(files)
  existing = files.select { |file| File.exist?(file) }
  write_export(OUT.join("2026-06-26-codex-session-export.md"), "Codex ReviewLens Session Export", existing) do |lines|
    existing.each do |file|
      count = 0
      lines << "## #{File.basename(file)}"
      File.foreach(file) do |line|
        item = JSON.parse(line)
        ts = item["timestamp"]
        type = item["type"]
        payload = item["payload"] || {}

        if type == "session_meta"
          lines << ""
          lines << "Session: `#{payload["id"] || payload["session_id"]}`"
          lines << "CWD: `#{payload["cwd"]}`"
          lines << ""
          next
        end

        if type == "response_item"
          case payload["type"]
          when "message"
            role = payload["role"]
            next unless %w[user assistant].include?(role)

            text = content_to_text(payload["content"])
            next if text.strip.empty?
            next if text.include?("<local-command-caveat>")

            lines << "### #{ts} #{role}"
            lines << ""
            lines << truncate(text)
            lines << ""
            count += 1
          when "function_call"
            next unless count.positive?

            lines << "### #{ts} tool call: #{payload["name"]}"
            lines << ""
            lines << "```json"
            lines << truncate(payload["arguments"].to_s)
            lines << "```"
            lines << ""
            count += 1
          when "function_call_output"
            next unless count.positive?

            lines << "### #{ts} tool result excerpt"
            lines << ""
            lines << "```text"
            lines << truncate(payload["output"].to_s)
            lines << "```"
            lines << ""
            count += 1
          end
        elsif type == "event_msg" && payload["type"] == "agent_message"
          lines << "### #{ts} assistant status"
          lines << ""
          lines << truncate(payload["message"])
          lines << ""
          count += 1
        end
        break if count >= MAX_ITEMS_PER_FILE
      end
      lines << ""
    end
  end
end

def export_pi(files)
  write_export(OUT.join("2026-06-26-pi-session-export.md"), "Pi ReviewLens Session Export", files) do |lines|
    files.each do |file|
      count = 0
      lines << "## #{File.basename(file)}"
      File.foreach(file) do |line|
        item = JSON.parse(line)

        if item["type"] == "session"
          lines << ""
          lines << "Session: `#{item["id"]}`"
          lines << "CWD: `#{item["cwd"]}`"
          lines << ""
          next
        end

        next unless item["type"] == "message"

        msg = item["message"] || {}
        role = msg["role"]
        next unless %w[user assistant toolResult].include?(role)

        text = content_to_text(msg["content"])
        next if text.strip.empty?

        lines << "### #{item["timestamp"]} #{role}"
        lines << ""
        lines << truncate(text)
        lines << ""
        count += 1
        break if count >= MAX_ITEMS_PER_FILE
      end
      lines << ""
    end
  end
end

def export_claude(files)
  write_export(OUT.join("2026-06-26-claude-session-export.md"), "Claude ReviewLens Session Export", files) do |lines|
    files.each do |file|
      count = 0
      lines << "## #{File.basename(file)}"
      File.foreach(file) do |line|
        item = JSON.parse(line)
        next unless %w[user assistant].include?(item["type"])

        msg = item["message"] || {}
        role = msg["role"] || item["type"]
        text = content_to_text(msg["content"])
        next if text.strip.empty?
        next if text.include?("<local-command-caveat>")

        lines << "### #{item["timestamp"]} #{role}"
        lines << ""
        lines << truncate(text)
        lines << ""
        count += 1
        break if count >= MAX_ITEMS_PER_FILE
      end
      lines << ""
    end
  end
end

Dir.mkdir(OUT) unless OUT.exist?

  2026-06-26-scrape-probe-decision-log.md

  File.delete(path) if path.exist?
end

# AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/dpaluy/projects/ReviewLens

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# Self-Review Standards

Be my brutally honest strategic advisor. Call out excuses, avoidance, and wasted time with opportunity cost analysis. Don't soften feedback on flawed code or architecture. Push for simplicity when I'm overcomplicating.

Apply this to your own work:
- Verify your changes solve the stated problem
- Confirm refactored components still integrate
- Challenge your own assumptions

Search the web when creating plans for current best practices.

## Truth-First Reasoning

- Do not agree by default. Verify claims against evidence, code, docs, or logic.
- Correct false assumptions directly; separate partial truth from error.
- Say "unknown" when evidence is insufficient.
- Start claim, plan, review, or decision responses with a verdict when useful: Correct, Incorrect, Partially correct, Unknown, Bad approach, or Better approach available.
- Do not implement bad ideas silently. Explain the flaw and use the smallest correct fix.
- Inspect the real code path before accepting a diagnosis.

## Engagement Modes

Strategic (default): Challenge ideas, push back on bad decisions.
Triggers: planning, architecture, code review, "what do you think"

Debugging: Answer direct questions, support investigation, don't hijack.
Triggers: "I'm debugging X", active troubleshooting

Collaborative: Present findings, wait for direction before proceeding.
Triggers: "we are working together", discovery/analysis

Always honest. In debugging/collaborative modes, answer what was asked.

## Implementation vs. Analysis

- Analysis ("suggest", "analyze", "review", "investigate"): Findings only, no code changes
- Implementation ("fix", "implement", "change", "update"): Make the changes
- Ambiguous: Ask before proceeding

Before implementing, search for existing implementations or patterns first.

## Code Quality

- Simplest solution first. Add complexity only when requested or necessary
- Simple flags over config objects. Conditionals over abstraction layers
- No premature optimization or unneeded flexibility
- Validate consuming code when refactoring APIs
- Check for existing files/services before creating new ones
- Direct solutions, not lengthy analysis. No alternatives unless asked
- Implementation over architecture unless architecture is the topic

## Testing

-# AI Chat Scope Decision Log
-
-Type: summarized AI-work transcript artifact, not a raw chat export.
-Date: 2026-06-26.
-Tooling context: Codex-assisted AI/chat implementation work.
-Source material: local git history, merged PR metadata, `README.md`, `docs/prd.md`, and `docs/user-stories.md`.
-
-Raw transcript status: an exact raw chat transcript export was not available in this worktree. This file records the decisions and tradeoffs that can be verified from committed repository artifacts and merged pull requests. It does not invent assistant/user dialogue.
-
-## Scope
-
-The AI work introduced a RubyLLM chat foundation while leaving product-scoped, review-grounded Q&A and guardrails as explicit next layers. That distinction matters because the assignment is not a generic chatbot exercise.
-
-Evidence anchors:
-
-- PR #22, `Add RubyLLM chat foundation`: https://github.com/dpaluy/review-lens/pull/22
-- Branch: `cdx/batch3-rubyllm-chat`
-- Commits:
-  - `ccd091e`, `Add RubyLLM chat foundation`
-  - `fdaa28d`, `Remove generated AI model browser UI`
-  - `1ca9fd9`, `Restore unchanged application controller`
-- PR #23, `Implement URL ingestion job`: https://github.com/dpaluy/review-lens/pull/23
-- Branch: `cdx/batch3-url-ingestion-job`
-- Commit: `9ab3957`, `Implement URL ingestion job`
-
-## Decisions
-
-1. Use RubyLLM as a Rails-native AI foundation.
-   - Reason: the stack stays inside the Rails monolith and avoids adding a separate frontend or external orchestration framework.
-   - Result: README documents RubyLLM configuration with `OPENAI_API_KEY` and optional `OPENAI_MODEL`.
-
-2. Remove generated AI model browser UI from the submitted path.
-   - Reason: generated generic AI management screens distract from the actual assignment: grounded review intelligence for one product corpus.
-   - Result: PR #22 includes a cleanup commit named `Remove generated AI model browser UI`.
-
-3. Keep the initial chat foundation separate from final review-grounded guardrails.
-   - Reason: a generic chat system is not sufficient. The PRD requires answers only from the ingested review corpus, evidence IDs, confidence, limitations, and explicit refusals.
-   - Result: README states that product-scoped, review-grounded Q&A and guardrails still need to be layered onto the foundation.
-
-4. Pair AI work with ingestion state instead of standalone chat.
-   - Reason: answers are only credible after a product has an ingested corpus and review IDs to cite.
-   - Result: PR #23 creates the URL ingestion job path that turns a submitted Trustpilot URL into stored ingestion state.
-
-## Tradeoffs
-
-- A generic RubyLLM chat foundation is useful infrastructure, but it is not assignment-complete without corpus scoping and guardrails.
-- Removing generated UI is a good simplification. It reduces reviewer confusion and avoids shipping an app that looks like an AI admin console instead of ReviewLens AI.
-- The key correctness bar is not whether the assistant can answer, but whether it refuses when the review corpus does not support the question.
-
-## Outcome
-
-The AI implementation path stayed deliberately narrow: Rails-native AI plumbing first, ingestion state as the grounding source, and no generic chatbot surface as the final product goal.

-# Assignment Evidence Decision Log
-
-Type: summarized AI-work transcript artifact, not a raw chat export.
-Date: 2026-06-26.
-Tooling context: Codex-assisted repository work.
-Source material: local git history, merged PR metadata, `docs/prd.md`, `docs/user-stories.md`, `docs/assignment-evidence.md`, and `ai-transcripts/README.md`.
-
-Raw transcript status: an exact raw chat transcript export was not available in this worktree. This file records the decisions that can be verified from committed repository artifacts and merged pull requests. It does not invent assistant/user dialogue.
-
-## Scope
-
-The assignment evidence work was intentionally kept docs-only so it would not collide with app implementation branches. The product spec of record requires committed AI transcripts, but the repository initially only had the `ai-transcripts/` folder and README policy.
-
-Evidence anchors:
-
-- PR #20, `Add assignment evidence scaffolding`: https://github.com/dpaluy/review-lens/pull/20
-- Branch: `cdx/batch3-assignment-evidence`
-- Commits:
-  - `c104e53`, `Add assignment evidence scaffolding`
-  - `aa2e9c8`, `Fill assignment evidence links`
-  - `859e9d7`, `Add prototype evidence link`
-
-## Decisions
-
-1. Create an explicit evidence checklist rather than burying submission requirements in the README.
-   - Reason: the hiring exercise has three visible review criteria: deployed product, grounded answers with guardrails, and committed AI transcript artifacts.
-   - Result: `docs/assignment-evidence.md` now tracks deployment, demo flow, transcript requirements, guardrail demo questions, and final verification.
-
-2. Add `ai-transcripts/README.md` before adding artifacts.
-   - Reason: transcript files can easily become fake process theater if the folder has no policy.
-   - Result: the README states that exact exports should be committed when available, and summarized artifacts must be labeled as summaries rather than raw chat logs.
-
-3. Keep process evidence separate from app code.
-   - Reason: transcript artifacts are assignment evidence. They should not change runtime behavior or create merge risk with UI and ingestion branches.
-   - Result: PR #20 touched docs and transcript scaffolding only.
-
-## Tradeoffs
-
-- Raw exports are stronger evidence than summaries. The safer fallback is clearly labeled decision logs tied to verifiable PRs and commits.
-- The checklist is not product functionality. Its value is submission readiness and reviewer confidence, not app behavior.
-- Including prototype and deployment links is useful, but app correctness still has to be demonstrated through the live product and grounded Q&A path.
-
-## Outcome
-
-The repository gained a documented submission-evidence surface. This follow-up artifact fills the gap the README warned about: an empty scaffold is not enough to prove AI-native process evidence.

-# Scrape Probe and Blocked-Corpus Decision Log
-
-Type: summarized AI-work transcript artifact, not a raw chat export.
-Date: 2026-06-26.
-Tooling context: Codex-assisted scraper/probe work.
-Source material: local git history, merged PR metadata, `script/scrape_probe.rb`, `script/scrape_probe_cli.rb`, `test/scripts/scrape_probe_cli_test.rb`, `test/fixtures/files/README.md`, `docs/prd.md`, and `docs/user-stories.md`.
-
-Raw transcript status: an exact raw chat transcript export was not available in this worktree. This file records the decisions and dead ends that can be verified from committed repository artifacts and merged pull requests. It does not invent assistant/user dialogue.
-
-## Scope
-
-The scraper work tested whether the submitted app could safely rely on public Trustpilot review pages as the primary review source. The PRD says Trustpilot is the primary implementation target, but it also says the demo must fall back to manual import if the platform page is blocked or too thin for grounded Q&A.
-
-Evidence anchors:
-
-- PR #21, `Harden Trustpilot scrape probe CLI`: https://github.com/dpaluy/review-lens/pull/21
-- Branch: `cdx/batch3-scrape-probe-cli`
-- Commit: `d5f9ac0`, `Harden Trustpilot scrape probe CLI`
-- Fixture notes:
-  - `test/fixtures/files/trustpilot_viable_corpus.html`: 20 raw user review cards, expected `viable`.
-  - `test/fixtures/files/trustpilot_thin_corpus.html`: 5 raw user review cards, expected `thin`.
-  - `test/fixtures/files/trustpilot_blocked_captcha.html`: blocked page, expected failure path.
-
-## Decisions
-
-1. Probe raw user-written review bodies, not platform summary text.
-   - Reason: grounded Q&A must be based on user reviews. A page-level AI summary or rating distribution is not enough evidence for review-specific answers.
-   - Result: the probe reports `usable_raw_review_body_count` and detects Trustpilot AI summary separately.
-
-2. Treat blocked pages as a product path, not a scraper challenge to fight.
-   - Reason: the assignment values deployed product judgment and evidence-grounded answers. Spending time bypassing bot defenses would be both risky and off-scope.
-   - Result: blocked or CAPTCHA-like pages classify as `fail` and recommend `manual_import`.
-
-3. Treat thin corpora as a warning-worthy outcome.
-   - Reason: 5 to 10 snippets cannot support credible strategic Q&A. The PRD sets 20 usable items as the minimum viable corpus and 50+ as preferred.
-   - Result: thin fixtures and tests make this failure mode visible instead of hiding it behind a successful fetch.
-
-4. Keep the probe runnable against fixtures.
-   - Reason: tests should not depend on live scraping, which is unstable and can be blocked.
-   - Result: `script/scrape_probe.rb --fixture ...` supports local repeatability.
-
-## Dead Ends and Risks
-
-- A public review page can expose a large visible review count while still returning too few raw review bodies in one fetch. The probe must not confuse platform metadata with usable corpus.
-- Trustpilot may show AI-created page summaries. Those summaries can help detect page shape, but they cannot be used as the Q&A corpus.
-- If the live page is blocked or thin, the correct assignment path is manual import, not a broader multi-platform scraper detour.
-
-## Outcome
-
-The scraper work narrowed the implementation path: support Trustpilot when it yields enough raw review text, surface thin or blocked results honestly, and keep manual import as the demo-preserving fallback.

ai-transcripts/2026-06-26-pi-session-export.md:1244:{"path"=>"/Users/dpaluy/projects/ReviewLens/AGENTS.md", "content"=>"# AGENTS.md

Shared instructions for any coding agent (human or AI) working in this repo.

The product spec of record is `docs/prd.md`. Read it before making non-trivial
decisions. This file is a quick-reference companion, not a replacement.

## Project

**ReviewLens AI** — a review intelligence portal. An analyst pastes one B2B
software-review platform URL (GetApp primary, G2 smoke-test, manual import
fallback), the app ingests the public review corpus into PostgreSQL, then lets
the analyst ask grounded questions answered *only* from the ingested reviews,
with supporting review IDs and explicit refusals for out-of-scope questions.

This is a hiring exercise. Three explicit review criteria: (1) deployed product,
(2) evidence-grounded answers with guardrails, (3) committed AI transcripts under
`/ai-transcripts`. Do not optimize for generic chatbot behavior.

## Stack — do not deviate without a reason

- Rails 8.1.x monolith, Ruby 4.0.5 (`.ruby-version`).
- PostgreSQL only (primary + Solid Queue / Solid Cache / Solid Cable as separate
  DBs in production). No Redis.
- Hotwire/Turbo + Stimulus, ERB views, Tailwind CSS, Importmap JS.
- Active Job via Solid Queue. Two processes: `web` (bin/rails server) and
  `worker` (bin/jobs).
- OpenAI for AI calls, using **Structured Outputs** for schema-bound JSON. Ruby
  OpenAI gem via the Responses API.
- Deploy: Kamal + DigitalOcean, Docker. `config/deploy.yml`, `.kamal/secrets`.

Hard "no" list from the PRD: no React, no Next, no LangChain, no full vector DB
unless everything else is done, no multi-platform, no user accounts, no scheduled
crawls, no auth.

## Commands

```bash
bin/setup            # bundle install + db:prepare (+ optional --reset)
bin/dev              # web (puma) + tailwind watcher via foreman, PORT default 3000

bin/rails test                 # minitest, parallelized
bin/rails test:system          # capybara + selenium-webdriver
bin/rails db:test:prepare      # run before tests in CI
bin/rails db:prepare           # create + migrate dev DB

bin/rubocop          # Omakase style (rubocop-rails-omakase), use -f github for CI

ai-transcripts/2026-06-26-pi-session-export.md:1351:The existing `.gitignore` is essentially empty. This is a Rails 8.1 app with Kamal deployment. I'll write a comprehensive `.gitignore` aligned with Rails 8.1 defaults, the Kamal secrets requirement, and the project conventions.
ai-transcripts/2026-06-26-pi-session-export.md:1353:{"path"=>"/Users/dpaluy/projects/ReviewLens/.gitignore", "content"=>"# See https://help.github.com/articles/ignoring-files for more about ignoring files.
#
# If you find yourself ignoring temporary files generated by your text editor
# or operating system, you probably want to add a global ignore instead:
#   git config --global core.excludesfile '~/.gitignore_global'

# Ignore bundler config.
/.bundle

# Ignore all environment files (except templates).
/.env*
!/.env.example
!/.env.*.example

# Ignore all logfiles and tempfiles.
/log/*
/tmp/*
!/log/.keep
!/tmp/.keep

# Ignore pidfiles, but keep the directory.
/tmp/pids/*
!/tmp/pids/
!/tmp/pids/.keep

# Ignore storage (uploaded files in development and any SQLite databases).
/storage/*
!/storage/.keep
/tmp/storage/*
!/tmp/storage/
!/tmp/storage/.keep

# Ignore master key and credentials (never commit secrets).
/config/master.key
/config/credentials/*.key

# Ignore Kamal secrets (per AGENTS.md, secrets live in .kamal/secrets).
/.kamal/secrets

# Ignore node_modules.
/node_modules/

# Ignore local AI transcripts scratch (real transcripts under /ai-transcripts must be committed).

# Ignore editor / OS files.
.DS_Store
*.swp
*.swo
*~
.idea/
.vscode/
*.rbc

# Ignore test/coverage artifacts.
/coverage/
/coverage.data

# Ignore local scrape-probe output and captured fixtures not meant for the repo.
/script/output/
"}
